critical_incidents.title: Admin account compromised after brute-force attack
critical_incidents.description: Account 'admin@corp.internal' received 2,847 failed login attempts from 185.220.101.44 between 02:55 and 03:13 UTC, followed by a successful login at 03:14:22 UTC from the same IP. Session established for 42 minutes. IP is a known Tor exit node.
raw_log_samples: Mar 10 02:55:01 web-01 sshd[29321]: Failed password for admin from 185.220.101.44 port 57234 ssh2
Mar 10 02:55:02 web-01 sshd[29322]: Failed password for admin from 185.220.101.44 port 57235 ssh2
Mar 10 03:14:22 web-01 sshd[31044]: Accepted password for admin from 185.220.101.44 port 58901 ssh2
Mar 10 03:14:22 web-01 sshd[31044]: pam_unix(sshd:session): session opened for user admin by (uid=0)

indicators.ip_reputation: Tor exit node / Brute-force source (AbuseIPDB score: 97)

critical_incidents.response_taken: Account disabled. Session terminated. Password reset initiated. IP blocked at firewall.
critical_incidents.recommendations: Implement account lockout after 5 failures. Enable MFA for all admin accounts. Use fail2ban or equivalent. Review all actions taken during the 42-minute session.

critical_incidents.title: Unusual large data transfer to external IP
critical_incidents.description: Database server 'payments-db-prod-01' (10.0.2.45) established outbound connection to 77.88.55.66:443 and transferred 4.7 GB over 18 minutes. This IP is not in the approved egress allowlist. CloudTrail shows S3 GetObject calls for entire 'payment-receipts-prod' bucket 3 minutes prior.
mitre_ttp: T1530 - Data from Cloud Storage Object
T1537 - Transfer Data to Cloud Account
critical_incidents.response_taken: Firewall rule added to block 77.88.55.66. S3 bucket access locked down. Incident declared. Legal hold initiated.
critical_incidents.recommendations: Implement strict egress filtering. Enable S3 Macie for sensitive data detection. Monitor for bulk S3 ListObjects/GetObject API calls. SIEM rule for large outbound transfers created.

critical_incidents.title: Unauthorized sudo usage leading to root access
critical_incidents.description: User account 'deploy-svc' executed a GTFOBins-based privilege escalation via sudo misconfiguration. User ran 'sudo find / -exec /bin/bash \;' exploiting an overly permissive sudoers entry. Root shell obtained on app-server-03.
raw_log_samples: Mar 10 22:47:01 app-03 sudo: deploy-svc : TTY=pts/0 ; PWD=/home/deploy-svc ; USER=root ; COMMAND=/usr/bin/find / -exec /bin/bash \;
Mar 10 22:47:03 app-03 su: (to root) deploy-svc on pts/0
Mar 10 22:47:05 app-03 useradd[48821]: new user: name=backdoor, UID=0, GID=0, home=/root/backdoor
critical_incidents.response_taken: Host isolated from network. deploy-svc account disabled. Backdoor account deleted. Cron job removed. Forensic image taken.
critical_incidents.recommendations: Audit all sudoers entries. Use principle of least privilege. Never allow NOPASSWD for programs capable of spawning shells. Run regular audits with sudoers linting tools.

high_alerts.title: Port scan detected from internal host
high_alerts.description: Host 10.0.4.87 scanned 4,200 internal IP addresses across ports 22, 80, 443, 3306, 5432 over 4 minutes. Consistent with nmap -sV behavior.
high_alerts.recommendations: Investigate 10.0.4.87 for compromise. Implement internal network segmentation. Alert on high-rate internal SYN patterns.

high_alerts.title: AWS IAM user accessing from unusual country
high_alerts.description: IAM user 'ci-deploy-prod' authenticated from 103.21.244.0 (China) at 04:55 UTC. Normal access pattern is US-East only. User made 14 API calls including DescribeSecurityGroups, ListBuckets, and DescribeInstances.
high_alerts.recommendations: Revoke and rotate IAM credentials. Enable MFA for all IAM users. Implement GuardDuty anomaly-based alerts. Review all API calls from this session.

high_alerts.title: CloudTrail logging disabled
high_alerts.description: CloudTrail trail 'prod-audit-trail' was stopped at 02:10:44 UTC by IAM user 'devops-user-07'. Trail was stopped for 14 minutes before being re-enabled. API calls during the gap are unaudited.
mitre_ttp: T1562.008 - Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
high_alerts.recommendations: Alert on StopLogging CloudTrail API calls. Protect trail with IAM condition denying StopLogging for all users. Use AWS Config rule cloud-trail-enabled.

log_misconfigurations.title: Nginx access logs missing key fields — no response time, upstream, user-agent
log_misconfigurations.description: Nginx log format missing $request_time, $upstream_response_time, $http_user_agent, and $http_x_forwarded_for. Insufficient for security investigation and performance triage.
log_misconfigurations.impact: Unable to identify client IPs behind load balancers. Missing user-agent data impedes bot detection and forensic analysis.

log_misconfigurations.title: Audit logging (auditd) not running on production hosts
log_misconfigurations.description: auditd service is not installed or not running on 3 of 6 production application servers. System call auditing absent. File access, user authentication, and privilege use not logged.
log_misconfigurations.detection_evidence: systemctl status auditd → Unit auditd.service could not be found
log_misconfigurations.impact: Cannot detect or reconstruct privilege escalation, file tampering, or unauthorized access events on these hosts.
log_misconfigurations.remediation: apt install auditd audispd-plugins; systemctl enable --now auditd; configure /etc/audit/rules.d/ with CIS Level 2 rules.

log_misconfigurations.title: Log rotation misconfigured — logs older than 24 hours deleted
log_misconfigurations.description: logrotate configured to keep only 1 rotation (1 day of logs). Security investigations often require 30-90 days of historical logs.
log_misconfigurations.remediation: Set rotate 90 to retain 90 days. For compliance: PCI DSS requires 12 months retention (3 months immediately available).
log_misconfigurations.impact: LOG-INC-2026031101 investigation hampered — 30 days of prior brute-force history unavailable.

log_misconfigurations.title: SSH login failures not forwarded to SIEM
log_misconfigurations.description: Filebeat configuration on bastion hosts excludes /var/log/auth.log from ingestion into Elastic SIEM. SSH brute-force attempts and successful logins from bastion are not visible in centralized logging.
log_misconfigurations.detection_evidence: Elastic: No auth.log events from bastion-01 or bastion-02 in last 30 days
log_misconfigurations.remediation: Add auth.log path to Filebeat config: paths: ['/var/log/auth.log', '/var/log/secure']. Restart filebeat service.
log_misconfigurations.impact: LOG-INC-2026031101 brute-force not detected via SIEM — discovered only during manual log review.

log_misconfigurations.title: CloudTrail not configured for all regions
log_misconfigurations.description: CloudTrail trail 'prod-audit-trail' is configured only for us-east-1. API activity in us-west-2, eu-west-1, and ap-southeast-1 is not logged. Attacker could provision resources in unmonitored regions.
log_misconfigurations.remediation: Enable 'Apply trail to all regions' in CloudTrail console or via Terraform: is_multi_region_trail = true. Ensure global service events (IAM, STS) are also captured.
log_misconfigurations.impact: Backdoor resources or malicious Lambda functions could be deployed in unmonitored regions without detection.

log_misconfigurations.title: Docker daemon not logging container events
log_misconfigurations.description: Docker daemon configured with log-driver 'none'. All container stdout/stderr discarded. Container activity, errors, and access patterns unavailable.
log_misconfigurations.remediation: Set log-driver to json-file or forward to centralized logging: {"log-driver": "json-file", "log-opts": {"max-size": "100m", "max-file": "5"}}
log_misconfigurations.impact: Runtime incidents (cryptominer, reverse shell) in containers cannot be investigated. Application errors invisible.

log_misconfigurations.title: Kubernetes API server audit logging disabled
log_misconfigurations.description: Kubernetes API server not started with --audit-log-path or --audit-policy-file flags. All API server actions (pod creation, RBAC changes, secret access) unlogged.
log_misconfigurations.detection_evidence: kubectl get pod kube-apiserver-prod-01 -n kube-system -o yaml | grep audit → no audit flags found
log_misconfigurations.remediation: Add --audit-policy-file=/etc/kubernetes/audit-policy.yaml and --audit-log-path=/var/log/kubernetes/audit.log to kube-apiserver manifests. Create audit policy with at least Metadata level for all resources.
log_misconfigurations.impact: Cannot detect malicious kubectl commands, RBAC escalation, or secret exfiltration via the Kubernetes API.

log_misconfigurations.title: S3 server access logging disabled on sensitive buckets
log_misconfigurations.description: Three production S3 buckets containing PCI-scoped and PII data do not have server access logging enabled. Object access cannot be audited.
log_misconfigurations.remediation: Enable logging on each bucket: aws s3api put-bucket-logging --bucket payment-receipts-prod --bucket-logging-status '{"LoggingEnabled":{"TargetBucket":"s3-access-logs","TargetPrefix":"payment-receipts-prod/"}}'
log_misconfigurations.compliance_impact: PCI DSS Requirement 10: Audit log access to cardholder data. This is a PCI compliance gap.
log_misconfigurations.impact: Data exfiltration from S3 (LOG-INC-2026031102) was detected only via VPC flow logs — S3 access logs would have provided earlier detection and full audit trail.

log_misconfigurations.title: Application logs contain sensitive data (PAN, passwords)
log_misconfigurations.description: Application logs contain unmasked Primary Account Numbers (PANs), partial passwords, and session tokens. Sample: 'Processing payment for card 4111111111111234, amount=$450.00'. Constitutes PCI DSS violation.
log_misconfigurations.raw_log_example: 2026-03-10 11:23:44 INFO PaymentProcessor - Charging card 4111111111111234 for user_id=8472 amount=450.00 cvv=382
log_misconfigurations.remediation: Implement log sanitization middleware. Mask/redact sensitive fields before logging. Use structured logging with allowlisted fields only. Review and purge existing logs.
log_misconfigurations.compliance_impact: PCI DSS Requirement 3.4: Render PAN unreadable. This is an immediate PCI DSS violation requiring remediation and potentially notification.
